reviews.review_msg: One of the 2 best profs I had at SFU..... 10 years later his teaching still sticks with me. Never worked so hard for an A but definitely rewarding.

Horrible prof. Very biased and played favorites. Asking him for help leaves you feeling even more confused than you were before and he is completely unorganized, you're lucky if you actually end up with the grade he originally told you you'd received.
I would agreed that he is hard and quite political... But NOT Arrogant!!!! He constantly jokingly says that he is semi-literate...

this professor is very motivating and helpful. he also have a greate way of getting students to interact during class, and really forces the students to think and learn. An enjoyable experience throughout the semester

This guy knows very little and is quite arrogant.  He tries to hide his knowledge deficits with humour and basically BS.  I would say he should stop teaching 442 but I don't think he would be capable of teaching any other marketing course.  Maybe leave the profession.  That's my advice...

DO NOT take BUS 449 with Gary Mauser. Class has nothing to do with business AT ALL, constantly change his criteria for marking. He "tries" very hard to be objective but fails everytime. I think it's time for him to retire.
A little one-sided in my opinion. Definitely NOT my fave prof.

Cant recall a single thing that was taught in lecture(lack of attendance and interest), but is one of only a select bunch of professors who teach you about life lessons.  Life lessons are extremely important in life, more so than any course or GPA can provide.
Great prof if you dont mind hard work. Funny, interesting, and very helpful. Biggest workload of any SFU Bus course, but well worth it.
awesome. extremely helpful and welling to flesh out ideas with you. one of my fav profs for sure.
Easy work, just lots of it!  If you are going into mktg, take it w gary for sure
Makes you work hard, but very helpful.
